svg.aria-label: Two gates around the Part 3 tool DAG. On the left, a prospective critic gate runs before execution: it checks the plan for an unknown tool, an unsatisfiable dependency, a dependency cycle, and a redundant step, then either rejects a bad draft or clears the good plan E1 to E4 for execution. On the right, the DAG executes: E1 lookup_status returns that SKU-ACME-EB is discontinued and permanent, E2 get_price raises a permanent error, and an error-triggered replanner rewrites only the remaining tail E2 and E3 from SKU-ACME-EB to the replacement SKU-GLX-EB while the completed node E1 is kept memoized. After the rewrite E2 returns 79 dollars, E3 a 2-year limited warranty, and E4 totals 93 dollars 22.
svg.title: Two gates around the DAG: a prospective critic before execution, an error-triggered replanner during it

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: Two gates around the DAG: critique the plan before it runs, replan only the tail when it breaks

svg.text: The critic catches the planner's structural mistakes up front; the replanner revises the not-yet-run subgraph when the world disagrees mid-run.

svg.text: Feeding an error back (Part 2) says the step failed; replanning is how a committed plan does something about it. A blind executor hits the same dead SKU and produces no valid quote.
